explicit_text: We live on a lovely lake here in Leon Park, and it’s a peaceful place that I cherish. My husband John and I enjoy our home here, and I often bring friends over for help and assistance. Leon Park is where we’ve built our life together, surrounded by nature and tranquility.

explicit_text: I was born in Bradford in the north of England about 55 years ago. It was a place where I grew up with five brothers and three sisters, and my father was an actor there. Bradford shaped much of my early life before I left England to pursue nursing and eventually come to the United States.

explicit_text: England is my homeland, where I spent my childhood and early nursing career. I trained at Blackburn Infirmary in Lancashire and worked in London before moving to the United States around 1904 or 1905. England’s customs, like tea drinking and Shrove Tuesday, stayed with me even after I settled abroad.

explicit_text: I started my nursing training at Blackburn Infirmary in Lancashire and graduated just before turning 21. It was a rigorous course, but I persevered and made a specialty of children’s cases there. This institution was foundational to my career in nursing before I moved on to work in London and later the United States.

explicit_text: I had a strong desire to come to the United States, seeing it as a land of opportunity unlike England. After working in London and following my father’s passing, I arrived here around 1904 or 1905. I took citizenship here despite family objections and made my life in places like New York and Florida, embracing American customs while sharing some English traditions.

explicit_text: I spent some time in New York City after arriving in the United States, nursing a child through a long illness. Although I didn’t stay long initially, about four to six months, I developed bronchial trouble later and returned to New York before moving on to Florida. New York was an important early stop in my American journey.

explicit_text: Shortly after I came to the United States, a brother and sister of mine moved to Canada. I visited them several times but didn’t find Canada particularly appealing compared to the US. This helped solidify my decision to take out American citizenship despite family disagreements.

explicit_text: After developing bronchial trouble, my physician advised me to spend the winter in Florida, so I came to Jacksonville. I liked Florida so much that I stayed there for several years. It became an important part of my life after my time in New York and helped improve my health.

explicit_text: Shrove Tuesday, or Pancake Tuesday as we called it in England, was a cherished tradition from my childhood. We always had pancakes made with milk, eggs, and oats flour, cooked thin like wafers and eaten with butter, sugar, and lemon juice alongside tea. I taught this custom to my friends when I first came to the United States, though over time I lost my status for it and eventually gave it up.

I taught this custom to my friends when I first came to the United States, though over time I lost my status for it and eventually gave it up.
